name: conductor-revert
description: Git-aware undo by logical work unit (track, phase, or task)
capabilities: Revert entire git tracks, specific phases, or individual tasks
Detect and report merge conflicts before they occur
Display full execution plan with affected files before any changes
Update plan.md and track status after successful reverts
Handle edge cases like already-reverted commits or unpushed changes
Require explicit YES confirmation before executing any git commands
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 59 static analysis findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' patterns are Markdown documentation examples, not executable code. The skill describes safe git revert operations with explicit safety rules forbidding dangerous commands like git reset --hard and git push --force. Requires explicit user confirmation before any changes.
valueStatement: Safely undo git commits by track, phase, or task with full history preservation. This skill provides structured revert workflows with explicit confirmation and conflict detection.
userTitle: Revert git changes by logical work unit
limitations: Requires git repository with Conductor tracks initialized
Cannot revert commits that do not follow the expected naming format
Requires manual intervention for merge conflicts
Only works with commits that reference track identifiers in messages

useCases.targetUser: Developers who need to safely roll back problematic changes
useCases.title: Undo a failed feature implementation
useCases.description: Revert all commits for a specific task that introduced bugs or did not meet requirements

useCases.targetUser: Team leads managing iterative development workflows
useCases.title: Restore previous working state
useCases.description: Revert an entire phase of work to return to a known good state before a major refactor

useCases.targetUser: Engineers who prototype features that may be abandoned
useCases.title: Clean up experimental work
useCases.description: Remove all traces of an experimental track that was not merged to production

promptTemplates.title: Revert a specific task
promptTemplates.prompt: Revert task 2.3 in the auth_20250115 track. Show me what commits will be affected and confirm before proceeding.
promptTemplates.scenario: You need to undo a specific task that introduced a bug

promptTemplates.title: Revert an entire phase
promptTemplates.prompt: Revert phase 2 of the dashboard_20250112 track. I want to see the full plan with all affected files before confirming.
promptTemplates.scenario: An entire phase of work needs to be rolled back

promptTemplates.title: Interactive revert selection
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me revert some recent work. Show me the available tracks and tasks I can revert.
promptTemplates.scenario: You are unsure which specific changes to revert

promptTemplates.title: Full track removal
promptTemplates.prompt: Revert the entire experiment_20250120 track. This experimental feature was not approved and needs to be fully removed from history.
promptTemplates.scenario: Removing all traces of an abandoned experimental feature

bestPractices: Always review the full execution plan before confirming any revert operation
Run tests after revert completion to verify the application works correctly
Communicate with your team before reverting shared or reviewed work
antiPatterns: Do not use this skill to hide mistakes from team members - discuss issues openly
Avoid reverting work that others have built upon without coordination
Do not skip the explicit confirmation step - always review the plan first

faq.question: What is the difference between git revert and git reset?
faq.answer: Git revert creates new commits that undo previous changes while preserving history. Git reset removes commits entirely, rewriting history. This skill uses only git revert for safety and audit trails.

faq.question: Can I revert commits that have already been pushed to remote?
faq.answer: Yes. Reverting pushed commits creates new revert commits that you can safely push. This is the recommended approach over force pushing, which can cause problems for collaborators.

faq.question: What happens if a merge conflict occurs during revert?
faq.answer: The skill halts immediately and displays conflict details. You must manually resolve conflicts before continuing. The skill preserves completed reverts and provides guidance for resolution.

faq.question: Can I undo a revert if I made a mistake?
faq.answer: Yes. You can run git revert on the revert commits to restore the original changes. The skill provides undo instructions in the verification output.

faq.question: What if the skill cannot find commits for my track?
faq.answer: This may mean the track was never committed, or commits used a different naming format. The skill will offer to delete the track directory or cancel the operation.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my plan.md automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. After successful git reverts, the skill updates plan.md to mark reverted tasks as pending. These changes are not committed - they reflect the new state of your work.
